heroDesc: Integrate Sakura Account into your app, enabling unified identity authentication in minutes
quickStartDesc: Follow a few simple steps to add OAuth2.0 / OIDC authentication to your app
step1Desc: Register your application in the Sakura Account dashboard to get a Client ID and Client Secret
step2Desc: Set your redirect URI to ensure the callback after authorization works correctly
step3Desc: Redirect users to the authorization page; after consent, you'll receive an authorization code
step4Desc: Use the authorization code to exchange for an Access Token and Refresh Token at the Token endpoint
step5Desc: Use the Access Token to call the UserInfo endpoint and retrieve the user's profile
featureOAuthDesc: Industry-standard authorization protocol supporting Authorization Code, Client Credentials, and more
featureOIDCDesc: Identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0 providing standardized user identity information
featureSSODesc: One login grants access to all authorized applications, improving user experience
featurePasskeyDesc: WebAuthn-based biometric and hardware key authentication—more secure and convenient
featureJWTDesc: JWT-formatted ID Tokens and Access Tokens with built-in signature verification
featureScopesDesc: Control app access to user data through scopes, following the principle of least privilege
authFlowDesc: Standard OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE flow
flowStep2: Browser redirects to Sakura Account authorization page
flowStep3: User logs in and grants consent
flowStep4: Authorization code returned via callback to your app
flowStep5: App backend exchanges auth code for tokens
flowStep6: Use tokens to retrieve user information
endpointsDesc: All endpoints return standard JSON and follow OAuth 2.0 / OIDC specifications
epAuthorizeDesc: User authorization entry point, supports OAuth 2.0 and OIDC requests
epTokenDesc: Exchange authorization codes, refresh tokens, or client credentials for tokens
epUserInfoDesc: Retrieve the authenticated user's identity information
epOpenIdConfigDesc: OIDC Discovery endpoint returning all related endpoints and metadata
epJwksDesc: JSON Web Key Set—public keys for verifying JWT signatures
epRevokeDesc: Revoke previously issued access or refresh tokens
authCodeDesc: The recommended secure authorization flow for apps with a backend
clientCredentialsDesc: For service-to-service and machine-to-machine authentication scenarios
refreshTokenDesc: Obtain a new Access Token after the current one expires using a Refresh Token
clientIdDesc: The Client ID obtained when registering your app
scopeDesc: Requested permission scopes, e.g. openid profile email. Optional — defaults to the app's registered base scopes when omitted. Must include openid if using OIDC.
stateDesc: Random string to prevent CSRF attacks. Required in all requests.
codeChallengeDesc: S256 hash for PKCE to prevent authorization code interception
codeChallengeMethodDesc: PKCE challenge method. Optional — defaults to S256 when omitted. Only S256 is accepted; plain is not supported.
promptDesc: Controls the authorization page behavior. Optional — if omitted, the system auto-determines whether to show the consent screen (skips if already logged in and previously authorized). Pass consent to force re-confirmation.
codeExamplesDesc: Integration examples in various languages and frameworks to help you get started
securityDesc: To protect your app and user data, please follow these security recommendations
secStateDesc: Generate a random state value and verify it in callbacks to effectively prevent CSRF attacks
secPKCEDesc: Even in apps with backend services, enabling PKCE adds an extra layer of security
secSecretDesc: Never expose your Client Secret in client-side code or public repositories—store it securely on the backend only
secValidateDesc: Verify ID Token signatures using JWKS public keys, and validate claims such as iss, aud, and exp
secHTTPSDesc: All communication must use HTTPS to protect tokens and user data in transit
secRotationDesc: Periodically update Client Secrets and signing keys to reduce exposure risk
scopesDesc: Control the scope of app access to user data through the scope parameter
scopeProfileInfo: Get profile information such as nickname and avatar
notePkce: PKCE (S256) is mandatory — code_challenge is required. code_challenge_method defaults to S256 if omitted.
noteRedirectUri: redirect_uri must exactly match the callback URL configured in your app settings
noteTokenExpiry: Default Access Token lifetime is 1 hour; Refresh Token lifetime is 30 days
faqDesc: Common questions and answers encountered during integration
faqQ1: Which OAuth 2.0 grant flows does Sakura Account support?
faqA1: We currently support Authorization Code, Client Credentials, and Refresh Token flows. PKCE is enabled by default for enhanced security.
faqQ2: How long is the Access Token valid? What happens when it expires?
faqA2: Access Tokens default to a 1-hour lifetime. After expiration, you can use the Refresh Token to obtain a new Access Token without requiring the user to re-authorize.
faqQ3: Can I customize the login page appearance?
faqA3: You can set a custom logo and primary color via optional OAuth request parameters. Contact us for more customization options.
faqQ4: Can Passkey authentication replace traditional passwords?
faqA4: Yes. Sakura Account supports WebAuthn/Passkey standards, allowing users to log in password-free with fingerprints, face recognition, or hardware keys—more secure than passwords.
faqQ5: How do I migrate existing users to Sakura Account?
faqA5: We provide user import APIs and progressive migration strategies. You can gradually migrate users while maintaining support for your existing account system.
faqQ6: Which programming languages have official SDKs?
faqA6: Currently we offer REST APIs—any OAuth 2.0 / OIDC compliant generic SDK works directly. Official SDKs are under development.
conclusionDesc: Register your first app and integrate within minutes
tryItDesc: Simulate the full OAuth authorization flow
simWelcomeSubtitle: Sign in to your account to manage identity and application access.
simAppDesc: A demo app using Sakura Account for authentication
simAppHint: Click the button above to experience the full OAuth authorization flow
simLoginHint: Enter any username and password to try
simScopeProfileDesc: Access your nickname, avatar and basic info
simDemoComplete: Demo complete — here is the user info returned to the app
simDeniedDesc: You denied the authorization request for this app
simPasskeyNotSupported: Passkey login is not supported in demo mode
